message:
fix(time-parser): return None on overflowing relative durations

parse_time_input used Duration::days/hours/minutes/weeks, which panic
when the multiplied nanoseconds overflow i64. Feeding parse_time_input
an input like "9223372036854775807d" (or its ago/negative variants)
therefore crashed the caller with a chrono panic instead of returning
None.

Swap the four construction sites for a single relative_duration(unit,
val) helper that uses Duration::try_days / try_hours / try_minutes /
try_weeks, each of which returns Option<Duration>. A sibling
subtract_duration_ms helper funnels the final arithmetic through
DateTime::checked_sub_signed, so even valid-looking durations that
overflow when subtracted from Utc::now() degrade to None instead of
panicking.

tests/regression_coding_agent_session_search_vmtms.rs covers the
regression: four oversized inputs (positive/negative i64::MAX in both
"Nd" and "N days ago" shapes) that previously panicked now return None.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
